name: Rhode Island (United States)
description: A white field charged with a gold anchor in the center, encircled by thirteen gold five-pointed stars; below, a blue ribbon scroll bears the word 'HOPE' in gold letters.
history: Adopted in 1897. The anchor and the motto 'HOPE' have been used by the colony and state since the seventeenth century — the colonial seal of 1647 already showed a fouled anchor. The thirteen stars were added on adoption to recall Rhode Island's status as one of the original thirteen colonies. Rhode Island's flag is unusually small in size — its short hoist is dictated by statute.

features.role: thirteen gold stars encircling the anchor
features.symbolism: Rhode Island as one of the original thirteen colonies

features.role: a fouled gold anchor at the center of the ring of stars
features.symbolism: Rhode Island's centuries-old emblem and seal device, attested from the colonial seal of 1647; the anchor symbolizes hope

features.role: a blue ribbon below the anchor inscribed with the state motto 'HOPE'
features.symbolism: the state motto, recorded since the seventeenth century
